tickerText: [TENNIS] Wimbledon 2026 main draw set: 44-year-old wildcard Serena Williams draws 20-year-old Australian Maya Joint (world No. 53) for a Tuesday, June 30, first-round blockbuster — her first Grand Slam singles match since 2022; defending champions Jannik Sinner and Iga Swiatek lead the fields as play begins Monday, June 29, at the All England Club • [SQUASH] Madrid Squash Open 2026 crowns a champion Saturday night at Club de Squash Tres Cantos — Czech qualifier Marek Panacek faces No. 5 seed Leandro Romiglio after Panacek upset top seed Henry Leung in the quarters and romped past Will Salter in the semis; Romiglio edged Spain's Sergio Garcia Pollan in five games • [BADMINTON] YONEX US Open 2026 semifinal Saturday at Titan Gym in Fullerton: 18-year-old Rounak Chouhan chases his maiden Super 300 final after stunning World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen and beating Misha Zilberman in the quarters; Devika Sihag and Tanvi Sharma headline an Indian surge into the penultimate round of the $250,000 BWF event • [PICKLEBALL] Portland PPA Challenger hits its midpoint Saturday at The Picklr Westbrook in Maine — pros and amateurs share courts across singles, doubles, and mixed divisions through Sunday; PPA Tour Asia shifts focus to the Sansan Tokyo Open, July 1–4, after Sahra Dennehy's Beijing Open double • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT United States Smash 2026 qualifying wraps Saturday at Ontario Convention Center with Sessions 3 and 4 deciding the last main-draw berths ahead of Sunday's Round of 64 — Wang Chuqin, Sun Yingsha, Tomokazu Harimoto, Felix Lebrun, Kanak Jha, and Lily Zhang headline the $1.55M Grand Smash through July 5

newsItems.title: Serena Williams Draws Maya Joint for Wimbledon Return
newsItems.excerpt: The Wimbledon singles draw ceremony on Friday, June 26, set Serena Williams' path back to Grand Slam singles: the 44-year-old seven-time champion, competing on a wildcard, will face 20-year-old Australian Maya Joint, ranked world No. 53, in the first round on Tuesday, June 30. It marks Williams' first major singles match since the 2022 US Open and her first Wimbledon appearance since losing to Harmony Tan that same year. Joint made her All England Club debut in 2025. Main-draw play across the grounds begins Monday, June 29, with defending champions Jannik Sinner and Iga Swiatek opening Centre Court action.

newsItems.title: Chouhan and Sihag Lead Indian Charge Into US Open Semifinals
newsItems.excerpt: The YONEX US Open 2026 moves into semifinal Saturday, June 27, at Titan Gym in Fullerton, California, with Indian teenagers Rounak Chouhan and Devika Sihag headlining the $250,000 Super 300 event. Chouhan produced the upset of the week by toppling World No. 6 and top seed Chou Tien Chen 21-17, 26-24 in the Round of 16 — his first win over a top-10 opponent — then beat Israel's Misha Zilberman to reach his maiden BWF Super 300 semifinal. Sihag advanced with straight-game wins over Thailand's Tonrug Saeheng and Japan's Riko Gunji. Kidambi Srikanth and Tanvi Sharma also reached the quarterfinals as India fields its deepest US Open contingent in years.

newsItems.title: Portland PPA Challenger Reaches Championship Weekend in Maine
newsItems.excerpt: The Portland PPA Challenger enters its second day Saturday, June 27, at The Picklr Westbrook in Westbrook, Maine, running through Sunday across men's and women's singles, doubles, and mixed doubles. Amateur brackets play alongside the Humana Championship Court where touring pros compete for one of the Challenger Series' northeastern stops on the 2026 calendar. Spectator tickets are $15 at the gate. The event follows a busy international stretch that included the Capital Securities Beijing Open, where Sahra Dennehy claimed women's singles and doubles titles; the PPA Tour Asia circuit heads next to the Sansan Tokyo Open, July 1–4.

newsItems.title: United States Smash Qualifying Closes as Main Draw Looms
newsItems.excerpt: WTT United States Smash 2026 qualifying concludes Saturday, June 27, at the Ontario Convention Center in Greater Los Angeles, with afternoon and evening sessions determining the final men's and women's singles berths before the main draw opens Sunday, June 28. The second Grand Smash of 2026 carries a $1.55 million prize pool across an 11-day run through July 5. Defending champions Wang Chuqin and Zhu Yuling return alongside Sun Yingsha, Tomokazu Harimoto, Felix Lebrun, and Hugo Calderano. Americans Kanak Jha, Lily Zhang, and Amy Wang are in the field with host-nation wild cards as table tennis builds its Southern California footprint ahead of LA28.
